commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.10.0: Complete all 4 phases — full feature build

Phase 1 — Stability & Correctness:
- Fix fade timer to use real delta time (arg1) instead of
  fixed POLL_INTERVAL decrement
- Filter resist/immune/miss/dodge/parry from timer detection
  so failed casts don't start timers
- Clean SPELL_DURATIONS: remove cast-time entries (Frostbolt,
  Fireball, Scorch), fix wrong values (Dark Pact), add type
  field ("cd" or "dur") to every spell
- Sync timer state with GetSpellCooldown on login/reload so
  cooldowns in progress are shown correctly

Phase 2 — UX & Config:
- Opacity slider in config panel (20%-100%)
- Auto-hide threat checkbox in config
- Threat warning sound toggle
- Timer ready sound toggle
- Per-character saved variables (ArcaneWatchCharDB) for panel
  positions and custom spell overrides
- Dynamic threat panel height: shrinks to visible rows

Phase 3 — Threat Meter Depth:
- Register raid-level combat log events
  (CHAT_MSG_*_FRIENDLYPLAYER_*) for 20/40-man raids
- Parse HoT ticks ("gains N health from Playername's Spell")
- Per-target threat tracking: separate threat tables per mob,
  switching on PLAYER_TARGET_CHANGED, clearing on mob death
- High-threat warning: red flash overlay on threat panel +
  sound when player reaches 90% of tank threat

Phase 4 — Polish:
- Minimap button (left-click: config, right-click: toggle)
- Tooltips on threat rows (name, %, raw threat)
- Tooltips on timer rows (spell name, type, duration)
- Sound alerts: igQuestFailed on threat warning,
  igPlayerInviteAccept on timer ready

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(observability): populate request/response bodies, headers, and logs on detail page (#477)

Three root causes fixed:
1. OTEL Java agent v2.11.0 silently drops Span.setAttribute() calls when
   trace context is propagated via traceparent header. Upgraded to v2.25.0
   and added RequestDataCaptureService that writes body/header data directly
   to OpenSearch emf-request-data-* index as a reliable parallel path.
2. Header capture env var names were wrong in K8s configmaps (fixed in
   homelab-argo, already pushed to main).
3. No logs were being shipped to OpenSearch — OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER was "none"
   and Jaeger V2 lacks an opensearch exporter for logs. Created custom
   OpenSearchLogAppender Logback appender that batches and writes logs
   directly to emf-logs-* index via HTTP bulk API.

Co-authored-by: Craig Klinker <craigklinker@velocityglobal.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 닉네임 기반 연결 기능 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Batch save_prefix mx.eval: 36 per-layer syncs → 1 batched call

KVCache in-place mutation requires mx.eval to materialize prefix
slices before buffer reuse, so eval cannot be removed entirely.
Batching all tensors into a single mx.eval(*all_tensors) reduces
GPU→CPU sync overhead from 36 round-trips to 1.

Also updated review1.md: KV cache fragmentation analyzed as non-issue
for MLX unified memory model.

commit.message:
feat: error recovery + resilience (QP-4) (#166)

Add complete loading → content → error lifecycle with retry to all async
surfaces. Every key page now degrades gracefully during outages.

- TanStack Query: retry 2x with exponential backoff, 30min gcTime for
  stale data persistence, auto-refetch on reconnect
- AsyncContent wrapper: thin component for query loading/error/retry
- ErrorBoundary: class component with Sentry reporting + retry
- Route error boundaries: discover, my-gov, engage with context messages
- NetworkBanner: offline detection via navigator.onLine + /api/health,
  auto-invalidates queries on reconnect
- EpochBriefing: replaced gray error text with AsyncContent retry
- DRep/SPO CommandCenter: added error state with retry (was blank)
- ProposalSentiment: added fetch error state with retry

commit.message:
Fix cinematic finish sequence — 4 bugs causing invisible reveal

- CRIT: reveal_container (z=40) was hidden behind flash_rect (z=50);
  raised to z=55 and fade flash_rect out at start of Phase 2
- CRIT: skip tween-kill used get_children() which never finds Godot 4
  SceneTree-managed tweens; switched to get_processed_tweens()
- HIGH: position.y += offset stacked on repeated calls; snapshot rest
  positions before offsetting and tween back to snapshots
- MED: 0→TAU spin with EASE_IN_OUT looked stationary; changed to
  TRANS_CUBIC EASE_OUT over 1.2s for visible deceleration

feat(compaction): add chunked compression to avoid TPM rate limits

- Add 2-second delay between chunks to prevent 429 errors
- Each chunk gets independent fileOps to avoid context pollution
- LLM automatically merges summaries using UPDATE_SUMMARY_PROMPT
- MAX_TOKENS_PER_CHUNK = 50,000 for safety margin

commit.message:
Fix critical end-to-end workflow bugs from QA testing

Resolved all 11 issues identified during Comet browser automation testing
of dummy patient "Aarav Mehta" (drsandee-020).

Bug Fixes:

1. XSS Validation - Fixed overly aggressive pattern blocking AI-generated markdown
   - Updated regex in schemas.py to use word boundaries (\bon\w+=)
   - Prevents false positives on phrases like "based on" while still catching onclick=, onload=

2. Non-functional AI Brain Icons - Added missing form IDs
   - templates/provisional_diagnosis.html: Added id="provisional-diagnosis-form"
   - templates/follow_ups.html: Added id="followup-form"
   - Enables JavaScript event handlers to attach properly

3. Character Length Validation - Increased Reference field max length
   - schemas.py: Increased from 5,000 to 10,000 characters
   - Accommodates comprehensive AI-generated clinical references

4. Treatment Plan Summary Generation - Fixed incorrect Firestore collection names
   - main.py: Corrected 6 collection name mismatches:
     * subjective_perspectives → patient_perspectives
     * subjective_assessments → initial_plan
     * pathophysiological_mechanism → patho_mechanism
     * chronic_disease_factors → chronic_diseases
     * objective_assessment → objective_assessments (plural)
   - Resolves "No summary generated" error

5. Follow-Up Date Field - Added ID for automation compatibility
   - templates/follow_ups.html: Added id="session_date"
   - Enables automation tools to reliably target the date picker

6. Missing Chief Complaint in Report - Fixed field name mapping
   - templates/patient_report.html: Check both present_history and chief_complaint
   - Database stores as present_history, template now handles both names

7. Provisional Diagnosis Not Documented - Resolved by form ID fix (#2)
   - Brain icons now functional, users can generate AI suggestions

Tested workflow impact:
- All AI brain icons now functional across all assessment pages
- Treatment plan summary generation works correctly
- Patient reports display complete data
- Automation-friendly form fields
- AI-generated content no longer triggers false XSS warnings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add px-mind cognitive loop daemon

Three-layer cognitive architecture giving the robot an inner life:

Layer 1 — Awareness (every ~30s): reads sonar + session state, detects
  transitions (someone_appeared, someone_left, long_silence, time_period_changed).
  Writes state/awareness.json.

Layer 2 — Reflection (on transition or every ~5 min): calls Ollama (qwen3:1.7b)
  with awareness + recent thoughts + history. Produces a thought with mood,
  action decision, and salience score. Appends to state/thoughts.jsonl (last 50).
  Auto-remembers high-salience thoughts to notes.jsonl.

Layer 3 — Expression (when reflection decides to act): dispatches to existing
  tools — tool-describe-scene (observe), tool-perform (greet), tool-voice
  (comment), tool-remember, tool-look. 60s cooldown between expressions.

Integration:
- voice_loop.py build_model_prompt() now injects last 3 thoughts + current mood
  into LLM context, giving conversations continuity across interactions
- claude-voice-system.md: rule 15 instructs Claude to match robot's mood
- Pauses automatically during active conversations (listening=true)
- Uses venv python (no GPIO); delegates hardware to bin/tool-* subprocesses
